questions.q: What kind of creature is Sampati in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sampati is a great vulture and the elder brother of Jatayu.

questions.q: Sampati is the brother of which famous bird of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sampati is the elder brother of Jatayu, the vulture who fought Ravana.

questions.q: What physical condition makes Sampati unable to fly?
options: He is too old to move
questions.explanation: Sampati is wingless, having had his wings burned away, so he cannot fly.

questions.q: What crucial piece of information does Sampati give the searchers?
options: The location where Sita is held captive
questions.explanation: Sampati tells the searching vanaras that Sita is held captive in Lanka across the sea.

questions.q: Across what does Sampati say Sita is held captive?
questions.explanation: Sampati reveals that Sita is held in Lanka across the ocean.

questions.q: In which island kingdom does Sampati say Sita is being kept?
questions.explanation: Sampati identifies Lanka, Ravana's island kingdom, as the place where Sita is held.

questions.q: Who are the searchers that Sampati helps with his information?
questions.explanation: Sampati gives his information to the band of vanaras searching for Sita.

questions.q: Hearing about whose death deeply affects Sampati when the vanaras speak?
questions.explanation: Sampati is moved to learn of the death of his brother Jatayu from the vanaras.

questions.q: Sampati's revelation chiefly helps which hero leap across the sea?
questions.explanation: His revelation that Sita is in Lanka leads to Hanuman's great leap across the ocean.

questions.q: What renowned quality of vision is associated with vultures like Sampati?
questions.explanation: Sampati possesses extraordinarily keen, far-reaching sight, able to see Sita far away in Lanka.

questions.q: How, according to tradition, did Sampati lose his wings?
options: They were burned shielding Jatayu near the sun
He was cursed by a sage to lose them
questions.explanation: Sampati's wings were scorched away when he shielded the younger Jatayu from the sun's heat while flying high.

questions.q: When Sampati first sees the starving vanaras, what does he initially intend toward them?
questions.explanation: The wingless Sampati at first regards the despairing vanaras as food sent to him.

questions.q: On which mountain do the searching vanaras encounter Sampati?
questions.explanation: The vanaras, despairing of their search, meet Sampati on the slopes of the Vindhya mountain.

questions.q: What were Sampati and Jatayu attempting to do when Sampati's wings were burned?
questions.explanation: As young birds, Sampati and Jatayu competed to fly up toward the sun, where the heat scorched Sampati's wings.

questions.q: Who was the vanara leader of the southern search party that met Sampati?
questions.explanation: Angada, son of Vali, led the southern search party that encountered Sampati.

questions.q: Whose name, spoken by the grieving vanaras, first makes Sampati pay close attention?
questions.explanation: When the vanaras lament and mention Jatayu, Sampati is stirred to learn of his brother.

questions.q: What does Sampati ask the vanaras to do for him in return for his help?
options: To take him across the sea
To help with the last rites for Jatayu
questions.explanation: On learning of Jatayu's death, Sampati asks the vanaras to help him perform funeral rites for his fallen brother.

questions.q: Roughly how far across the sea does Sampati say Lanka lies?
questions.explanation: Sampati declares that Sita and Ravana lie a hundred yojanas across the ocean in Lanka.

questions.q: Whom does Sampati say he saw carrying Sita away through the sky?
questions.explanation: With his keen sight Sampati had seen Ravana carrying the weeping Sita through the air toward Lanka.

questions.q: How did the vanaras initially feel before Sampati gave them hope?
options: Despairing and ready to fast to death
questions.explanation: Having failed in their search and fearing Sugriva's wrath, the vanaras sat in despair, resolved to fast to death, when Sampati appeared.

questions.q: Sampati's father is named in tradition as which figure?
questions.explanation: Sampati and Jatayu are described as sons of Aruna, the charioteer of the sun.

questions.q: What does Sampati say will happen because he has heard Rama's name and helped the search?
options: His burned wings will grow back
He will be reborn as a vanara
He will rule over the birds
questions.explanation: Tradition relates that after aiding Rama's cause, Sampati's wings miraculously grow back.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, which sage's hermitage was near where Sampati lived, having foretold his role?
questions.explanation: The sage Nishakara consoled the fallen Sampati and foretold that he would one day aid Rama's messengers.

questions.q: What had the sage Nishakara promised would restore Sampati's wings?
questions.explanation: Nishakara told Sampati that helping those who sought Sita would cause his lost wings to regrow.

questions.q: Who is Sampati's son, mentioned as relaying news of Ravana to him?
questions.explanation: Sampati's son Suparshva brought him food and reported the sight of Ravana carrying Sita toward Lanka.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after losing his wings where did Sampati fall to earth?
questions.explanation: His wings burned, Sampati fell upon the Vindhya range, where he remained for ages until the vanaras found him.

questions.q: Why did Sampati's son Suparshva delay bringing him food on the day he saw Ravana?
options: He had been wounded by hunters
He had stopped to watch Ravana carry Sita past
He had quarreled with another bird
He had flown to Lanka by mistake
questions.explanation: Suparshva returned late because he had paused, blocked from his prey, while watching Ravana fly past carrying Sita.

questions.q: What relationship do Sampati and Jatayu bear to the great eagle Garuda in tradition?
options: They are of Garuda's kindred lineage of birds
questions.explanation: As sons of Aruna, brother of Garuda's father Vinata's line, Sampati and Jatayu belong to the same noble lineage of great birds.

questions.q: What does Sampati declare about his eyesight that lets him locate Sita so precisely?
options: A vulture can see prey from a hundred yojanas
He can see only what is near
His sight returns only at night
He needs the sun to see
questions.explanation: Sampati explains that vultures possess sight reaching a hundred yojanas, enabling him to see Sita far away in Lanka.

questions.q: After the vanaras heard Sampati, which of them was finally chosen to leap across the sea to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Encouraged by Sampati's news, the vanaras chose Hanuman, whose strength was roused by Jambavan, to leap the ocean to Lanka.
